# SRC008 - Media Reactions 2025

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Digital Discovery |
| Authors / source | Kantar |
| Year | 2025 |
| Source type | Industry media receptivity report |
| Quality | Medium |
| URL | [https://www.kantar.com/inspiration/advertising-media/media-reactions-2025-where-do-people-prefer-advertising](https://www.kantar.com/inspiration/advertising-media/media-reactions-2025-where-do-people-prefer-advertising) |

## Bias / limitation

Commercial media-research product; useful for directional channel receptivity.

## Core takeaway

Ad receptivity depends heavily on media environment.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use for channel/context matching: do not judge an ad apart from where it appears.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.

## Research extraction template

```markdown
### Source extraction

- Source ID: SRC008
- What it changes about our strategy:
- What it proves:
- What it does not prove:
- Ad tactic suggested:
- Landing page proof suggested:
- Measurement implication:
- Follow-up source needed:
```
